_meta.author: scorecard-refine-national autopilot
_meta.note: Evidence-finalize FL House Polk/Osceola batch (HD 47-51): paula-a-stark, jon-albert, jennifer-jonsson, jennifer-canady, hilary-holley. Evidence-first, every TRUE/FALSE cited; null where no evidence. High-value catch: Stark (R) is a documented maverick (voted AGAINST 2023 6-week ban, NO on 2026 fetal-personhood, one of 5 Rs against the local-gov DEI ban) and was disqualified from the 2026 ballot (Form 6).

profile.background: Newspaper publisher (ex-Osceola News-Gazette); Exec. Director, St. Cloud Main Street. In office since 2022. Catholic, moderate/maverick Republican.
profile.confidence_note: Evidence-finalized 2026-06-20 from cited votes/trackers (FL House, choicetracker, Florida Phoenix, Equality FL). DEVIATION RECORD: documented breaks from the GOP on life and DEI. Disqualified from the 2026 ballot (Form 6 financial-disclosure deficiency, June 2026); remains seated through the current term.

paula-a-stark@FL.notes_set: Catholic R, HD-47 (St. Cloud/Osceola), in office since 2022. DOCUMENTED CROSS-PARTY DEVIATIONS: (1) one of ~7 House Republicans who voted AGAINST the 2023 six-week abortion ban (HB7/SB300) and on record favoring a 15-week limit ('I said I would not go less than 15 weeks... we should have stayed there'); (2) voted NO on the H0289 fetal-personhood / wrongful-death bill (Jan 2026); (3) one of only 5 Republicans to vote AGAINST the local-government DEI ban (SB1134/HB1001), joining all Democrats. CANDIDACY: disqualified from the 2026 re-election ballot in June 2026 over a Form 6 financial-disclosure deficiency (exploring legal action); two Democrats are the only qualified candidates. Still the seated HD-47 rep through the current term. Other issue areas (2A, immigration, elections, federalism, economic) returned NO EVIDENCE -> null (not inferred from party).

0.note: Voted NO on H0289 fetal-personhood bill (Jan 2026); on record favoring a 15-week limit rather than personhood/conception.

1.note: One of ~7 House Republicans who voted AGAINST the 2023 six-week abortion ban (HB7/SB300); does not advocate abolition (favors 15 weeks).

4.note: One of only 5 Republicans to vote AGAINST the local-government DEI ban (SB1134/HB1001), joining all Democrats; opposed restricting LGBTQ/DEI programs in public policy.

profile.background: USMC veteran (~10 yrs); MBA (Chaminade Univ. of Honolulu); business owner; former Mayor of Frostproof (2020-2024). Freshman, sworn in Nov 2024. HD-48 = Polk County (Frostproof/Winter Haven/Lake Wales).
profile.confidence_note: Evidence-finalized 2026-06-20. Freshman (sworn in Nov 2024) -> NOT in office for the 2023 marquee gun/abortion votes. Scored from a HARD 2025 immigration roll call, FRTL + NRA-PVF endorsements, and cited iVoterGuide self-statements; unestablished cells null.

jon-albert@FL.notes_set: Freshman R, HD-48 (Polk Co.), sworn in Nov 2024 -> absent from the 2023 HB7 (6-week ban) and HB543 (permitless carry) votes. HARD: voted YEA on CS/SB 2-B (2025 'TRUMP Act' immigration enforcement, House 82-30); endorsed by Florida Right to Life PAC and NRA-PVF. Cited self-statements (iVoterGuide candidate survey): opposes elective abortion except life-of-mother, supports school choice/parent-directed funding, opposes mandatory service requirements violating religious conviction, requires voter photo ID, opposes gun restrictions/supports armed trained teachers. No cross-party deviation found; iVoterGuide grades him 'Conservative (Conditional)'.

0.note: Endorsed by Florida Right to Life PAC; per iVoterGuide survey opposes elective abortion except when the mother's life is at risk.
iVoterGuide survey: supports school choice and parent-directed education funding.

1.note: iVoterGuide survey: government should not close churches; opposes mandatory service requirements that violate religious beliefs.
iVoterGuide survey: requires voter photo ID; opposes the National Popular Vote Interstate Compact.

2.note: Voted YEA on CS/SB 2-B (2025 immigration enforcement / anti-sanctuary 'TRUMP Act' package), House 82-30, Jan 28 2025.

0.note: NRA-PVF endorsed; iVoterGuide survey opposes gun restrictions (FL has permitless carry).

1.note: iVoterGuide survey: opposes gun restrictions; supports trained teachers carrying firearms in schools.

profile.background: Full name Jennifer Kincart Jonsson. Businesswoman; B.S. Health Science + M.P.H. (UF). Freshman, sworn in Nov 2024. HD-49 = Polk County (SW Polk, south of Lakeland).
profile.confidence_note: Evidence-finalized 2026-06-20. Freshman (2025-); strongest HARD point is cosponsoring + voting YES on HB 289 (2026) fetal-personhood. Other cells scored from cited campaign self-statements or null.

jennifer-jonsson@FL.notes_set: Freshman R, HD-49 (Polk Co.), sworn in Nov 2024 -> absent from the 2023 votes. HARD: cosponsored AND voted YES on CS/HB 289 (2026) 'Civil Liability for the Wrongful Death of an Unborn Child' (fetal personhood), House passed 76-34, Jan 15 2026; endorsed by Florida Right to Life. Cited campaign self-statements: back law enforcement, deport illegal aliens / secure the border, empower parental involvement in education, cut taxes. No cross-party deviation found (individual 2025 roll calls beyond HB289 not separately retrievable -> those cells null, not inferred).

0.note: Cosponsored and voted YES on CS/HB 289 (2026) fetal-personhood / wrongful-death-of-an-unborn-child bill (House 76-34); Florida Right to Life endorsed.
Campaign platform: publicly backs law enforcement ('allow state and local law enforcement to do their job').

1.note: Campaign platform: deport illegal aliens and secure the border.

profile.background: Speaker-designate of the FL House (presumptive, would be first woman to lead the chamber). Member, Covenant Presbyterian Church, Lakeland; directed the RISE Institute at Lakeland Christian School 15 yrs; former public-school teacher. Married to FL Supreme Court Justice Charles Canady. In office since 2022. HD-50 = Lakeland/Polk Co.
profile.confidence_note: Evidence-finalized 2026-06-20 from HARD sponsorship/votes (FL House, Florida Politics, Freedom Index, LegiScan). Solidly conservative with a few documented limited-government deviations (Article V convention, a scholarship-expansion vote, fertility-preservation coverage, the budget). Cells with no isolated individual roll call (marriage/sexuality, elections, public justice) left null rather than inferred from party.

jennifer-canady@FL.notes_set: Senior R, HD-50 (Lakeland/Polk), in office since 2022; Speaker-designate. HARD: prime House sponsor of the 2023 six-week abortion ban (HB7 'Heartbeat Protection Act'); voted FOR the anti-ESG 'Combating ESG Activism' act and the 2A Financial Privacy Act; voted YES on SB1718 (2023 anti-sanctuary + E-Verify); voted to lower the firearm-purchase age 21->18; NRA-PVF + Florida Family Action endorsed. NOTED DEVIATIONS (Freedom Index, scored non-conservative but not cleanly mapped to a rubric cell -> left null, documented here): voted AGAINST an Article V Convention application, voted AGAINST expanding refundable tax-credit scholarships, voted FOR state-employee fertility-preservation coverage and the ~$117.5B budget. Declined the iVoterGuide questionnaire. Marriage/sexuality + elections + public-justice cells had no isolated individual roll call -> null.

0.note: Prime House sponsor of HB7, the 2023 six-week 'Heartbeat Protection Act' abortion ban.

4.note: Voted FOR the 'Combating ESG Activism' act (and the 2A Financial Privacy Act) -- opposes ESG capture of state finance.

2.note: Voted YES on SB1718 (2023) -- anti-sanctuary provisions / federal-enforcement cooperation.

3.note: Voted YES on SB1718 (2023), which expanded mandatory E-Verify for employment.

0.note: NRA-PVF endorsed; majority bloc for permitless carry; voted to lower the firearm-purchase age 21->18.

1.note: Voted FOR the 2A Financial Privacy Act and to lower the firearm-purchase age; NRA-PVF endorsed -- opposes new gun restrictions.

profile.background: Executive Director, Florida FFA Foundation; cattle rancher (Polk City). B.S. Agricultural Education & Communications (UF). Seated March 24, 2026 via special election (replaced Josie Tomkow); sworn in April 28, 2026. HD-51 = northern Polk County.
profile.confidence_note: Evidence-finalized 2026-06-20. Seated March 2026 -> essentially no roll-call record; scored only from clear cited campaign self-statements (school choice/homeschool, backing law enforcement). All other areas NO EVIDENCE -> null. Revisit after the 2027 session. NOT the GA progressive activist 'Hillary Holley' (different person).

hilary-holley@FL.notes_set: Freshman R, HD-51 (northern Polk Co.), seated March 24 2026 / sworn April 28 2026 -> too late for any 2023-2026 Regular Session social-issue roll calls; near-empty HARD record. Cited campaign self-statements only: strengthen educational choices / charter & homeschool options; back law enforcement with the resources they need. NO EVIDENCE FOUND on abortion, marriage/sexuality, 2A, elections, federalism (all null). Establishment-lane endorsements (FL Chamber, ag/leadership figures); no NRA/SBA/FL Right to Life endorsement found. Identity confirmed distinct from the Georgia 'Hillary Holley' (Care in Action) activist.

0.note: Campaign platform: strengthen educational choices for parents, increase access to charter schools and homeschooling options.
Campaign platform: back law enforcement officers with the resources they need to keep communities safe.
